title: Modernization under King Zog I
text: Albania, and especially Tirana, entered a period of modernization and westernization through political reform, urban transformation, and the work of local and foreign architects.
items: Tirana entered a phase of political and cultural transformation after becoming capital.
King Zog I promoted modernization and westernization.
Austrian and Italian architects were invited to work in Albania.
Albanian architects educated abroad contributed to modern architecture.
Florestano di Fausto promoted Novecento-style architecture.
Main cities saw civic construction influenced by European late historicist architecture.
Legislative reforms modernized finance, health, expropriation, and administration.
Social reforms included measures on dress codes and women’s emancipation.

title: Italian occupation and architectural consolidation
text: Under Italian Fascist rule, Albania underwent political regime change while the architectural and planning imprint of Italy deepened, especially in Tirana.
mapCaption: Political occupation and stronger Italian planning influence
items: Albania was annexed by Italy and placed under Fascist rule.
The Italian architectural imprint on Tirana deepened.
Central ministries and institutional buildings continued to reflect state-building ambitions.

title: Soviet-oriented socialist transformation
text: After 1945, Albania was transformed into a socialist state aligned with the Soviet Union. Planning, architecture, and education increasingly followed Soviet models.
mapTitle: Tirana in the early socialist period
mapCaption: Soviet influence on architecture and urban planning
items: Communists came to power and confiscated private property.
Albania became politically and ideologically aligned with the Soviet Union.
Moscow-trained Albanian architects introduced Soviet planning models.
Gani Strazimiri became a key figure in Stalinist architecture.
The 1956 plan for central Tirana proposed monumental axes, super-blocks, and socialist realist geometry.
Architectural education and references became strongly Soviet-oriented.

title: Standardization, institutes, and ideological control
text: The socialist period institutionalized design through centralized institutes, Soviet norms, standardized housing, and the ideological use of architecture and art.
mapCaption: Centralized planning and repetitive urban form
items: Architects and engineers were grouped into Design Institutes.
Town planning followed Soviet norms for workplaces, services, and facilities.
Standardization produced repetitive housing types and urban monotony.
Socialist realism was imposed across architecture and the arts.
Art and architecture became instruments of propaganda.
Large industrial and planning projects were presented as socialist achievements.

title: Mature socialist urban and construction development
text: During the mature socialist period, urban development continued through standardized expansion, specific urban examples in Tirana, and industrialized building systems such as prefabrication.
items: Durrës Street in Tirana is referenced as an example of socialist urban development.
The Camus prefabrication system was introduced in Albania.
Socialist visual culture appeared in magazines and architectural drawings.

title: Post-socialist transition and reinterpretation
text: After the collapse of dictatorship, socialist heritage entered a difficult transition marked by destruction, politicized debate, and only later renewed research interest.
items: There was little serious theoretical debate on socialist architecture after the regime fell.
Discussion became politicized and descriptive rather than scientific.
Factories and socialist heritage were damaged, stolen, or destroyed.
Albania’s fragile democracy pursued development and modernization.
In the last decade, research interest in socialist-period architecture has increased.

title: Brasini Masterplan for Tirana
description: Armando Brasini proposes a monumental boulevard and axial urban structure for Tirana.

title: Construction of Socialist Housing Blocks
description: Expansion of standardized housing blocks during socialist urbanization.

title: Post-Socialist Informal Expansion
description: Rapid informal expansion in peri-urban areas following the collapse of the socialist system.

title: Collaborative Urban Planning Project - Skanderbeg Square Development
description: Joint project between Gani Strazimiri and Klement Kolaneci for the development and modernization of Skanderbeg Square under socialist urban planning guidelines.
